age_limit.age_details: Maximum 30 years as on the last date of application
age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 30 years as on the last date of application. Age Relaxation: As per Government of India norms for SC/ST and Retired/superannuated government employees.

qualification.education_qualification: B.Tech. / B.E. / M.Sc. (or equivalent) in Physics / Engineering Physics / Applied Physics / Photonics / Photonics Engineering / Optics / Quantum Technology / Biomedical Engineering with 60% aggregate marks or 6.0 CGPA or equivalent marks AND Qualified in at least one of the following: CSIR-UGC NET (JRF/LS) / GATE / DBT-BET / INSPIRE Fellowship / Any other national-level fellowship (recognized by DST / DBT / UGC / CSIR).
qualification.details: B.Tech. / B.E. / M.Sc. (or equivalent) in Physics / Engineering Physics / Applied Physics / Photonics / Photonics Engineering / Optics / Quantum Technology / Biomedical Engineering with 60% aggregate marks or 6.0 CGPA or equivalent marks Qualified in at least one of the following: CSIR-UGC NET (JRF/LS) / GATE / DBT-BET / INSPIRE Fellowship / Any other national-level fellowship (recognized by DST / DBT / UGC / CSIR). Desirable Experience: Quantum optics and photonics / Lasers and nonlinear optics / Optical instrumentation and precision alignment / Interferometry and photodetection / Data acquisition and control systems; Scientific programming (Python, MATLAB, LabVIEW) / Image processing and computational imaging / Machine learning and AI for scientific applications / Microscopy and bio-imaging / FPGA, embedded systems, or instrumentation electronics. Additional Desirable Qualification (but not essential): M. Tech. / M. E. (or equivalent) in Physics / Engineering Physics / Applied Physics / Photonics / Photonics Engineering / Optics / Quantum Technology / Biomedical Engineering with 60% aggregate marks or 6.0 CGPA or equivalent marks.

salary_details.details: Junior Research Fellow (JRF): Rs. 37,000 per month (Consolidated)

selection_process: Selection/Intimation letter Verification of original documents
and other relevant testimonials at the time of joining.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online on or before 30-06-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of IIT Mandi to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the IIT Mandi Junior Research Fellow (JRF) position?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your completed application for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) position at IIT Mandi is 30th June 2026. Ensure you submit your application well before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the JRF post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a B.Tech./B.E./M.Sc. (or equivalent) in Physics, Engineering Physics, Applied Physics, Photonics, Photonics Engineering, Optics, Quantum Technology, or Biomedical Engineering with at least 60% marks or 6.0 CGPA. Additionally, they must have qualified in CSIR-UGC NET (JRF/LS), GATE, DBT-BET, INSPIRE Fellowship, or any other recognized national-level fellowship.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Junior Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) position is 30 years as of the last date of application. Age relaxation will be provided as per Government of India norms.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for the JRF post?
faq.answer: The selected candidate for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) position will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 37,000 per month.

faq.question: Q5. How can I apply for the IIT Mandi JRF recruitment?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online through the link provided in the 'Important Links' section. You will need to fill out the application form and upload necessary documents.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q7. What is the nature of the job?
faq.answer: This is a project-based research job at the Indian Institute of Technology Mandi.

faq.question: Q8. What are the desirable qualifications and experience for the JRF post?
faq.answer: Experience in quantum optics, photonics, lasers, optical instrumentation, scientific programming (Python, MATLAB, LabVIEW), image processing, AI, microscopy, or embedded systems is desirable. An M.Tech./M.E. degree is also considered an additional desirable qualification.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I find the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: The direct download link for the official notification PDF is available in the 'Important Links' section of this article.

faq.question: Q10. What documents do I need to submit with my application?
faq.answer: Your CV should include personal details, academic qualifications, research/project experience, publications, mobile number, and contact information for two referees. You will also need to upload your GATE score card, CSIR UGC-NET certificate, DBT-BET fellowship certificate, INSPIRE fellowship certificate, or any other relevant national-level fellowship certificate.
